downstream_consumers: LLOYD (Python; reads .coderef/foundation-docs-meta.json on every pre-prompt assembly to compute doc_age_seconds per Q5 section 5)
Future ROUTER pre-prompt assembly (per ROUTER-DESIGN.md section 11.2 Gate D)

risks.title: Existing foundation-docs may have inconsistent mtimes (regenerated in batches over seconds)
risks.mitigation: Schema accepts up to ~1 minute spread between min/max; LLOYD takes max for the most-aggressive freshness check.

risks.title: Atomic write race if two close-out paths fire simultaneously
risks.mitigation: Temp+rename atomic on POSIX same-FS; on Windows fs.renameSync is atomic same-volume. LLOYD's read is also resilient (try/except returns None on parse fail).

risks.title: sha256 content_hash adds non-trivial cost on large docs
risks.mitigation: Foundation-docs are <50KB total; hashing is sub-ms. Single full-pipeline run already takes >30s.

risks.title: Schema URL points to coderef.dev which doesn't host schemas yet
risks.mitigation: Use $schema URI as forward-compatible identifier only; LLOYD reads schema_version field as the version pin (per Q5 section 6 open Q2).

risks.title: Step 4.5 META insertion may collide with parallel SKILL.md edits in another WO
risks.mitigation: This is the only WO touching that skill today; merge conflicts unlikely.

dependencies: Q5 deliverable (DOC-AGE-EXPOSURE-Q5.md) for schema and write-point spec
Existing doc-gen pipeline (must run AFTER enhance-existing-docs.js so doc mtimes are stable)

decision_records.rationale: Per Q5 section 4 — co-located with other LLOYD-readable .coderef/ machine artifacts; matches LLOYD existing access pattern.
Per Q5 section 1.2 — relative values written at gen time freeze wrong from the moment they land. Computed-on-read by LLOYD.

decision_records.choice: Producer is /generate-foundation-docs skill final META step
decision_records.rationale: Per Q5 section 4 — only place with global view + trigger context. Individual doc-gen scripts only see one doc.

decision_records.choice: Trigger context via env vars (CODEREF_META_TRIGGER_KIND etc), default 'manual'
decision_records.rationale: Caller (close-workorder, chokidar daemon when shipped) sets env to attribute regen cause; allows zero-arg manual invocation.

decision_records.choice: Self-validate against bundled schema before write
decision_records.rationale: Schema drift detected at write time, not at first LLOYD read; cheaper diagnostic.
Per Q5 section 4 - LLOYD reads on every request; partial writes corrupt pre-prompt assembly.
